chief_complaint: An 84-year-old patient presented with atherothrombotic ischemic stroke and later experienced an embolic ischemic stroke.
history: The patient had severe left ventricular systolic dysfunction due to dilated cardiomyopathy and an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator in primary prevention. The patient was hospitalized for atherothrombotic ischemic stroke treated conservatively and later by carotid thromboendarterectomy for ulcerated plaque of the right internal carotid artery.
exam: No specific vital signs or physical examination findings are provided.
labs: The patient had an ulcerated plaque of the right internal carotid artery and thrombosis of the left atrial appendage without atrial fibrillation.
correct_diagnosis: Ischemic stroke due to thrombosis of the left atrial appendage
differentials: Cardioembolic stroke due to atrial fibrillation
Ischemic stroke due to thrombosis of the left atrial appendage
Stroke due to carotid artery dissection
correct_ranking: Ischemic stroke due to thrombosis of the left atrial appendage
Cardioembolic stroke due to atrial fibrillation
Stroke due to carotid artery dissection
